result.summary: Found 5 visual issues on this BetRivers Pennsylvania casino page, ranging from truncated UI elements to cut-off game card content that may confuse users.

issues.description: The navigation tab labeled 'Hot Ga...' is visibly cut off at the right edge of the screen, making the tab label unreadable.
issues.suggestion: Ensure navigation tabs are fully visible or implement a horizontally scrollable tab bar with a visible scroll indicator so users know more tabs exist.

issues.description: Game cards in the third column (e.g., 'MAGNI PHO...' and 'MUNC MI...') are clipped at the right edge with no scroll indicator, making the truncation look like a broken layout rather than intentional carousel behavior.
issues.suggestion: Add a visible scroll/swipe affordance (e.g., arrow indicator or partial peek effect with fade) to clearly communicate horizontal scrollability to the user.

issues.description: In the 'Top Played New Games' section, the third column cards ('HYPERO PYRAMIDO' and 'DAR WATE THE CURS') are similarly cut off with game titles truncated mid-word.
issues.suggestion: Apply the same fix as above — add a fade gradient or arrow indicator on the right edge to signal scrollable content.

issues.description: Game titles like 'MAGNI PHO', 'MUNC MI', 'HYPERO PYRAMIDO', and 'DAR WATE THE CURS' are truncated mid-word, making them unreadable without interaction.
issues.suggestion: Either widen the visible card area, use a tooltip/hover state, or ensure at minimum the first word of the game title is fully visible.

issues.description: Truncated navigation labels and game titles may be problematic for screen readers and users relying on readable text to identify content.
issues.suggestion: Ensure all interactive elements have fully descriptive aria-labels or alt text, even if the visible label is truncated due to layout constraints.
